codeReview.summary: Commit `6e4a8f5` does complete the runtime-message line-count fix itself: it removes `tests/unit/suites/runtime-messages.test.ts`, registers five replacement suites in `tests/unit/run-unit-tests.ts`, and each new runtime-message suite is under 200 lines (`core` 161, `validation` 52, `streaming` 42, `images` 86, `session` 52). However, compared with the prior failed rerun commit `b6b1367`, this fix repeats the same blocking scope problem by bundling unrelated version bumps in `package.json` and both extension manifests, so it does not adequately resolve the prior scrutiny failure overall.

issues.description: Although the runtime-message test split is correct, commit `6e4a8f5` also bumps the package version from `0.4.64` to `0.4.65`. Mission AGENTS explicitly forbids package/manifest version bumps in non-release refactor features, so this rerun repeats the same blocking scope violation that failed `fix-test-file-line-limits`.

The same runtime-message fix commit also bumps the Chrome manifest version from `0.4.64` to `0.4.65`. That release-metadata change is unrelated to splitting `runtime-messages.test.ts` and remains a blocking scope-discipline failure.

The Firefox manifest version is bumped in commit `6e4a8f5` as well, again introducing forbidden release metadata into a test-only refactor. Because the rerun repeats the prior review's version-bump problem, blocking issues remain.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `refactor-worker` skill's completion contract still is not being enforced reliably. This rerun handoff sets `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true`, but the transcript skeleton shows `npm run test:unit`, `wc -l`, `npm run check:repo-standards`, and a commit flow only; it does not show the skill-required `npm run lint`, `npm run build`, or `npm run test:integration` steps before completion.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/Users/sero/projects/browser-ai/.factory/skills/refactor-worker/SKILL.md:90-130` requires final validation plus accurate `followedProcedure` reporting; `/Users/sero/.factory/missions/fdd24bf3-fc74-45b3-a646-4179615c1e31/handoffs/2026-03-11T02-09-59-313Z__fix-runtime-messages-line-count__115cd7c6-f694-41fb-9069-b4f3d5e7a6ba.json:26,102` records only repo-standards and `followedProcedure: true`, and the session transcript for worker `115cd7c6-f694-41fb-9069-b4f3d5e7a6ba` contains no `npm run lint`, `npm run build`, or `npm run test:integration` commands.

summary: The runtime-message suite split itself is sound and removes the remaining oversized test file, but scrutiny still fails because commit `6e4a8f5` repeats the same forbidden package/manifest version bumps seen in the prior failed rerun, so the original scope-discipline blocker is not actually resolved.
